# Pre-registration: growth-of-structure predictions ahead of DESI DR2 full-shape

**Date frozen:** July 9, 2026
**Status:** Pre-registered BEFORE the DESI DR2 full-shape / growth cosmology
papers (expected later in 2026) and before any Euclid or Rubin growth results.
**Generated by:** `15_prereg_growth_predictions.py` (machinery adapted from
`14_growth_fsigma8_check.py`; standalone, no data files).
**Model under test:** ECF-MD14, smooth dark energy (no DE perturbations),
background w(z) only — the published model exactly as fit, at the certified
full-Planck constraint tau_ecf = 2.405 +0.87/−0.51 (68%), best fit 2.194.

---

## Why this is pre-registered

Growth of structure is this model's declared soft spot: the published analysis
is background-level, with dark energy perturbations neglected. DESI DR2
full-shape data will test growth at percent-level precision. Writing the
predictions down before those papers appear is the only way the comparison
carries evidential weight. Project history note, recorded for honesty: an
earlier qualitative pre-registration of growth *suppression* was wrong in sign
(documented in NOTE.md Addendum); the quantitative machinery below supersedes
it and reproduces the note's published +1.5% sigma8 figure at tau = 2.10.

## What is being predicted

All predictions at **fixed early-time (primordial) amplitude**, i.e. anchored
to the CMB; ratios are relative to LCDM at its own joint best fit
(Om = 0.3022), with ECF at its joint best fit (Om = 0.312).

**P1 — fsigma8(z) ratio at the DESI tracer effective redshifts.**
ECF predicts *enhanced* growth relative to LCDM, rising with redshift:

| z_eff | tau=1.894 | tau=2.194 | tau=2.405 | tau=3.277 |
|------:|----------:|----------:|----------:|----------:|
| 0.295 | 1.0053 | 1.0129 | 1.0171 | 1.0288 |
| 0.510 | 1.0045 | 1.0122 | 1.0165 | 1.0284 |
| 0.706 | 1.0086 | 1.0157 | 1.0196 | 1.0304 |
| 0.920 | 1.0146 | 1.0206 | 1.0239 | 1.0330 |
| 1.317 | 1.0237 | 1.0276 | 1.0297 | 1.0355 |
| 1.491 | 1.0260 | 1.0291 | 1.0308 | 1.0354 |

(Columns: 68% lower edge, full-likelihood best fit, posterior median, 68%
upper edge of the certified tau posterior.)

**P2 — sigma8 today.** At fixed early amplitude, sigma8(ECF)/sigma8(LCDM) − 1:

| tau | full (Om = 0.312) | DE-history only (Om matched) |
|----:|------------------:|-----------------------------:|
| 1.894 | +1.27% | +0.56% |
| 2.194 | +1.61% | +0.91% |
| 2.405 | +1.80% | +1.10% |
| 3.277 | +2.32% | +1.63% |

**Direction statement (the risk, stated up front):** the model predicts
*more* late-time growth than LCDM. This direction does not alleviate — and
mildly deepens — the S8 tension, and it opposes the mild growth-suppression
trend some full-shape analyses have reported. If the data move that way at
sufficient precision, this model loses. That is the point of writing it down.

**P3 — no ECF/CPL discrimination expected at growth level.** As established
in the published analysis, ECF and CPL are nearly degenerate in growth
observables at current precision; DESI DR2 full-shape is expected to
discriminate ECF from LCDM (marginally) but not ECF from CPL. The
CPL-unmimickable signature remains a w(z)-curvature target (kill condition 3),
not a growth target.

## Pass / tension / fail criteria (frozen now)

Let sigma8_data be the DESI DR2 full-shape constraint at fixed CMB amplitude
(or the equivalent published fsigma8 per tracer), and let the ECF band be the
68% tau band above.

- **PASS:** published growth constraints consistent with the ECF band within
  2 sigma (combined data + prediction-band uncertainty).
- **TENSION (reported, not spun):** 2–3 sigma below the ECF band. To be
  recorded in the repo verbatim regardless of outcome.
- **FAIL:** more than 3 sigma below the ECF band. Interpretation, frozen now:
  this falsifies the *smooth-DE ECF background* at growth level. Because the
  approximation and the model are tested jointly, the pre-committed follow-up
  is the DE-perturbation calculation (the named long-game item); if perturbed
  ECF cannot reconcile the growth data either, the model is disfavored, and
  the note will say so.
- An *upward* surprise (data above the band by >3 sigma) equally counts
  against the model and will be recorded identically.

## What we commit NOT to do

- No refitting of tau, Om, or H0 to growth data. Growth is a consistency
  check; the tau measurement remains distance-only. If growth data are ever
  promoted to fitting inputs, that is a new, separately pre-registered
  analysis with its own kill conditions.
- No post-hoc reweighting of which tracers or redshift bins "count." The
  table above is the complete prediction set.
- No substitution of the comparison target: the primary referee is the DESI
  collaboration's own published full-shape constraints, not reanalyses.

## Known caveats (led with, per constitution)

1. **Smooth-DE approximation.** For w(z) crossing −1, DE perturbations are
   formally required (PPF-type treatment); neglecting them is a percent-level
   systematic on growth in the worst case — the same order as the signal
   predicted here. This is why FAIL above triggers the perturbation
   calculation rather than instant model death. Honest ranking: this is the
   largest theory uncertainty in this pre-registration.
2. **Pipeline mismatch.** DESI full-shape analyses assume w0waCDM with PPF
   perturbations; mapping their published constraints onto smooth-DE ECF
   involves the CPL projection of the tau locus. At the precision of P1–P2
   this mapping error is subdominant, but it is not zero.
3. **Erratum flag for v2.0 (recorded here, frozen files untouched):** NOTE.md
   Addendum states the +1.5% sigma8 shift decomposes as "≈1.0% from Om,
   ≈0.5% from the dark-energy history." The generating machinery reproduces
   the +1.5% total exactly (τ = 2.10: +1.51%) but computes the split as
   ≈0.75% (Om) / ≈0.81% (DE history), with a small negative interaction term.
   The total — the physically meaningful number — is unaffected; the
   parenthetical split in the frozen note is inaccurate and should be
   corrected in the next content release.
4. The RSD compilation cross-check in script 14 (shape chi2 5.7/5.9/6.1 for
   ECF/CPL/LCDM on 7 points) remains the current-data baseline: no
   discrimination at present precision. The predictions above are where that
   changes, or doesn't.

## Analysis plan when DESI DR2 full-shape lands

1. Record the published growth constraints verbatim in this repo (new file,
   dated), before any comparison is computed.
2. Compute the comparison against the frozen table above; classify
   PASS / TENSION / FAIL per the criteria; commit the verdict regardless
   of outcome.
3. If TENSION or FAIL: open the DE-perturbation calculation as the next
   gated work item. If PASS: no update to the model, one line added to the
   note's validation trail at the next content release.
